title: Class and table method surface via add-method / replace-code / remove-method / display+table methods
instruction: In the sandbox model, create a class DemoCalcOps with a declaration only (no methods in the initial create). Then shape its method surface EXCLUSIVELY through d365fo_file action=modify operations: (1) add-method 'public int multiply(int _a, int _b)' with a stub body returning 0; (2) replace-code on multiply so it returns _a * _b (the golden verifies the final source text, so the stub must not survive); (3) add-method 'public static DemoCalcOps construct()' returning new DemoCalcOps(); (4) add-method tempScratch (any signature), then remove-method tempScratch (it must NOT exist in the final class). Separately, create a table DemoCalcData with fields ItemName (string, Name EDT) and Qty (real, Qty EDT), then via modify: (5) add-table-method a static 'find' method selecting firstonly by ItemName; (6) add-display-method 'displayDoubleQty' returning 2 * this.Qty (display method, Qty EDT return type). Both objects must build clean and validate_code(mode="syntax") must pass on every generated method before it is written.
